english: to be a deputy, representative, substitute Cs.; rigs (rigs) otsób-pa ('tshob pa) to be the first-born male in a family, the support of a family Dzl.; otsób-par ('tshob par) byéd-pa (byed pa) to substitute, to put in the place of another Dzl.; yduṅ (gdung) otsób-po ('tshob po) resp. for first-born Dzl.

pf. and fut. btsol (btsol), imp. tsol (tshol), W. *tsál-če* (tsal ce), 1. to seek, to search, to make research; tabs (thabs) to think upon means. -- 2. to try to obtain, zas (zas); to procure, acquire Mil.; to fetch Thg.
1. the letter sounding dz; cf. the observations to ཙ (tsa) tsa (tsa). -- 2. numerical figure: 19.
1. v. dza-ti (dza ti). -- 2. dzá-brduṅ-ba (dza brdung ba) to break through Sch.
prop. ཛཱ་ཏི་ (dzA ti), Ssk. जाती, nutmeg Lt. and vulgo; sometimes dza (dza) for it, po. Lt.
Lex. a hollow tree Sch.

1. Sch.: 'muddy deposit, green slime in the water'. -- 2. C. the markings of wood, speckled and variegated, in consequence of a disease of the tree, cf. lbá-ba (lba ba). -- 3. n. of an ancient king of China Glr.
more accur. ཛཱ་ལནྡྷ་ར་ (dzA lan d+ha ra) dza-lán-dha-ra (dza lan dha ra), n. of a province in the Punjâb, now 'Jellundur'.
čui (chu'i) dza-lu-ka (dza lu ka) Sch. 'water-spider'; in Ssk. however: leech.
prob. to be spelt rdza-bra (rdza bra) q.v.

gen. odzám-bu ('dzam bu), जम्बु, the rose apple-tree, Eugenia, which figures also in mythology; dzám-bui (dzam bu'i) glíṅ (gling), dzam-bu-glíṅ (dzam bu gling), dzam-glíṅ (dzam gling), जम्बुद्वीप, acc. to the ancient geography of India and Tibet, that part of the world which comprizes these countries, the triangular peninsula of Hindostan, occasionally including the immediate border-lands; but as in Brahman and Buddhist literature all that does not belong to these two religions is considered as not existing, or at least as hardly human, odzam-bu-glíṅ ('dzam bu gling) is simply used for earth, world. and odzam-bu-glíṅ-pa ('dzam bu gling pa), for inhabitant of the world, man.

also dzám-bha (dzam bha), Glr. the Tibetan Plutos, god of riches, = rnam-tos-srás (rnam thos sras), also rmugs-odzín (rmugs 'dzin) Lex., ynod-odzín (gnod 'dzin), and acc. to Schf.'s conjecture (Tar. 6, 1) also yñód-pa-čan (gnyod pa can); dzam-sér (dzam ser) this god painted yellow, dzam-nág (dzam nag) painted black Cs.
num. figure: 49.
Ssk. n. of a Buddhist scholar.
num. figure: 79.
Hindî: shoe C., W.
C. *dhsub-dhsúb jhè-pa* (dhsub dhsub jhe pa) to wag, to whisk the tail, of horses and cattle.
num. figure: 109.
C. *dhse-tse* (dhse tse), vent-hole for the smoke, chimney.
num. figure: 139.
Mil., Wdṅ., vulg. for yó-gi (yo gi), v. rnál-obyor-pa (rnal 'byor pa).
(Lex. = mtúṅ-pa (mthun pa)) to love, as friends or kinsmen do, kyo-śúg (khyo shug)
